holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC ripped +10.5% today and +14.4% over five days on what looks like an Apple-chip-deal rumor, but there's no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor the move — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the queue here. A rumor-driven gap of this size without a confirming press release is the textbook setup where stretched longs get faded; trimming strength locks in the unverified pop while leaving a core for if the Apple story actually prints.
INTC.bull: If the Apple foundry win gets formally confirmed, today's gap is the start of a re-rating, not the end.
INTC.bear: Rumor fades without confirmation and the +14% five-day move round-trips before any dated catalyst arrives to defend it.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — next earnings isn't in the data, and the only thing ahead is triple witching ~88 days out, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Tape is constructive (up 2.77% on the day, +2.69% on the week at $210.38), so there's no urgency to act either direction without a print or guide to lean on. Wait for an earnings date to populate before framing an add/trim window.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not company-specific)
NVDA.bull: Price action is firm with no overhang from a near-term print to navigate.
NVDA.bear: No dated catalyst means no defined edge; a stretched tape can mean-revert on any AI-spend headline.

ORCL.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for ORCL right now — next earnings hasn't been provided/confirmed, and the stock is essentially flat (+0.17% over 5 days) near $184, so there's no clear add or trim window forced by an event. The only thing on the horizon is triple witching ~88 days out, which is a market-structure event, not an Oracle catalyst. Hold the line and wait for the next earnings date (typically mid-Sept for ORCL's fiscal Q1) to firm up before pressing either direction.
ORCL.bull: AI/OCI capacity story and RPO growth keep working; flat tape near highs means buyers are absorbing supply ahead of the next print.
ORCL.bear: No near-term catalyst to re-rate the name; a stalled tape into a quad-witch window can shake out crowded longs if AI-infra sentiment cools.

NOK.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NOK right now — no confirmed earnings or ex-div date in the queue — so there's no specific window to lean into. The position is soft (-2.3% today, -4.3% on the week) at $13.49, which is uncomfortable but not actionable without a catalyst to fade against. Triple witching on Sep 18 is a tape event, not a NOK event; it doesn't change the thesis here.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro tape event, not NOK-specific)
NOK.bull: Weakness without a binary catalyst could set up a better entry if the network/AI infra narrative reasserts.
NOK.bear: Drifting lower with no near-term catalyst to rerate the name — slow bleed risk on a small position.

NBIS.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NBIS — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the window, and the only thing ahead is triple witching on 2026-09-18, which is a market-structure event, not a name-specific one. With a 1-share position and no price context provided, there's no real edge to lean either way; the job here is to wait for an actual NBIS-specific date (earnings confirmation, AI capacity/customer announcement, or sell-side event) before defining an add or trim window.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no confirmed NBIS catalyst)
NBIS.bull: AI infra demand and Nebius's GPU capacity ramp keep the structural story intact while we wait for the next print.
NBIS.bear: High-beta AI infra name with no near-term dated catalyst means flows and macro (incl. Sep witching) drive the tape, not fundamentals.

KO.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for KO — next earnings and ex-div aren't in the queue, and the only thing ahead is triple witching nearly three months out, which doesn't move a defensive staple like this. The -3.2% over five sessions on a 5-share position is noise, not a setup; without a print or dividend date to anchor an add/trim window, there's nothing to lean into. Re-rate once an earnings date or ex-div confirms.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific catalyst on calendar)
KO.bull: Defensive cash-flow compounder with dividend support; mild weakness in a staple typically gets bought before the next print.
KO.bear: Without a dated catalyst, drift can continue on rate/FX moves and there's no event to force a re-rate either direction.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — the first real catalyst window will be the debut earnings print once the company sets a date, and until then there's no event to trade against. The only thing on the calendar is Sep 18 triple witching, which can amplify volatility in newly-listed, thinly-floated names but isn't a fundamental catalyst. Best posture is to let the first reporting date get put on the tape before committing to an add/trim plan.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (only dated event on file; first SpaceX earnings date not yet published)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity premium in a marquee post-IPO name; first earnings could re-rate the float higher if Starlink/launch cadence numbers impress.
SPCX.bear: No confirmed catalyst dates, lockup mechanics unknown, and triple witching can whip a small-float new listing around on flow alone.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
